title: Market Brief — Wednesday, May 27, 2026
summary: GPU availability is loosening fast across every class. Util-avail stands at 37% for A100, 28% for H100, 44% for H200, and 28% for B200, meaning no tier is near scarcity and H200 is approaching oversupply. B200 spot crashed 18.7% day-over-day to $4.17/hr while H100 dropped 16.3% to $2.44/hr, signaling a demand cliff as utilization fell double digits across the board over 30 days.
paragraphs: GPU availability is loosening fast across every class. Util-avail stands at 37% for A100, 28% for H100, 44% for H200, and 28% for B200, meaning no tier is near scarcity and H200 is approaching oversupply. B200 spot crashed 18.7% day-over-day to $4.17/hr while H100 dropped 16.3% to $2.44/hr, signaling a demand cliff as utilization fell double digits across the board over 30 days.

At TCO-adjusted cost (1.25x spot), running Llama 70B on H100 costs roughly $8.90/M tokens, making DeepSeek V3.2 at $0.45/M output a staggering loss of about $8.45 per million tokens. Meta Llama 3.3 70B at $0.32/M output is equally underwater. OpenAI gpt-oss-120b at $0.18/M is the most aggressive loss leader in the stack. Grades: OpenAI Neutral, Anthropic Strong, Google Neutral, DeepSeek Burning Cash.

B200 at $4.17/hr spot with 63% of offerings sitting idle and 72% Ornn utilization is overvalued relative to H100 at $2.44/hr delivering comparable inference margins at high tier. Buy H100 spot for Llama-class workloads and sell any locked B200 capacity above $4.50/hr before the utilization floor drops further.
bullets: 28% util-avail on H100 means 72% of tracked compute is occupied, but that figure fell 19.7 percentage points in 30 days.
44% util-avail on H200 is the loosest GPU class, with utilization down 26.2 percentage points month-over-month.
$4.17 median B200 spot is down 18.7% in one day, the largest single-session GPU price drop in this dataset.
21.78 LLMflation index means frontier token pricing has fallen roughly 78% from the GPT-4 launch baseline of 100.
$0.18 per million output tokens on OpenAI gpt-oss-120b is the cheapest closed-model pricing ever recorded.
193.7% annualized volatility on H200 OCPI makes it the riskiest GPU contract to hold on any term commitment.
